question_text: Which statement correctly describes the arrangement of particles in a solid?

options.label: A
options.text: Particles are loosely packed and move freely throughout the material

options.label: B
options.text: Particles are tightly packed in a fixed arrangement and vibrate in fixed positions

options.label: C
options.text: Particles are spread far apart with no forces between them

options.label: D
options.text: Particles are arranged in layers that can slide over each other easily

correct_answer: Particles are tightly packed in a fixed arrangement and vibrate in fixed positions
explanation: In solids, particles are held together by strong attractive forces in a fixed, ordered arrangement. Although the particles vibrate slightly due to thermal energy, they cannot move from their fixed positions, which is why solids have a definite shape and volume. This is different from liquids (where particles can move past each other) and gases (where particles move freely).
